headline: Corning Stock Is Falling. Why J.P. Morgan Disagrees With the ‘Blue-Sky Scenario.’
summary: Shares of  Corning  declined Thursday after  J.P. Morgan  analysts downgraded the artificial-intelligence beneficiary with the firm cautious heading into earnings.  A J.P. Morgan analyst team, led by Samik Chatterjee, downgraded  Corning  to Neutral from Overweight.  The firm, however, raised its price target on Corning stock to $175 from $115.

headline: Niron Magnetics Appoints Mike Brommer as Chief Operations Officer to Advance Manufacturing Capacity
summary: MINNEAPOLIS, April 16, 2026--Niron Magnetics, the leading manufacturer of rare-earth-free Iron Nitride permanent magnets, today announced the appointment of Mike Brommer as Chief Operations Officer. Brommer will lead the company’s operational strategy as it accelerates its commercial growth and commissions its first state-of-the-art permanent magnet manufacturing plant in Sartell, Minn., which broke ground in September 2025 and is expected to produce 1,500 tons of Iron Nitride magnets annually.

headline: AAR, UFP Industries, Schneider, Arrow Electronics, and Corning Shares Plummet, What You Need To Know
summary: A number of stocks fell in the afternoon session after news of a potential Middle East ceasefire triggered a major shift in the stock market. For weeks, investors held defensive and energy stocks during the conflict between the U.S. and Iran.

headline: Corning Taps Meta And Solar To Power AI Infrastructure Growth
summary: Corning (NYSE:GLW) has expanded its role as an AI infrastructure supplier through a new fiber optic partnership with Meta Platforms. The company is also investing in U.S. solar wafer manufacturing, strengthening its presence in the domestic solar supply chain. These moves connect Corning directly to both hyperscale data networks for AI and the buildout of U.S. solar capacity. For anyone tracking Corning, the story now goes beyond specialty glass into core infrastructure for AI and clean...
